front: Figure: this president authorized the dropping of the atomic bombs on Hiroshima and Nagasaki
Figure: this "failed haberdasher" became president following the death of Franklin Roosevelt
Figure: this president was first elected to the Senate with the help of what corrupt Missouri political machine?
Figure: in the contested 1940 Senate primary, this politician was supported by Robert Hannegan after former Governor Lloyd Stark challenged him
Figure: this politician led a namesake Senate committee that exposed fraud and waste in WWII military contracts
Work: this essay by G.E.M. Anscombe protested Oxford University awarding an honorary degree to what president, arguing his atomic bomb decision amounted to murder?
work: The Atomic Bomb and Its Aftermath

front: Figure: this president's eponymous "Doctrine," laid out in a 1947 speech to Congress, pledged support for anticommunist forces worldwide and led toward NATO
Figure: this president's decision to recognize the state of Israel in 1948 was influenced by his former business partner, Eddie Jacobson
Figure: this president ended an Iran Crisis by pressuring the Soviets to withdraw support from a separatist Azerbaijani government
Figure: this president repudiated his predecessor's promise of support to the Saudi king, citing a lack of Arab constituents
Figure: this president gave Saudi Arabia a foreign tax credit called the "Golden Gimmick," splitting oil revenues 50/50 with American companies
Figure: under this president, the US supported a coup against Syrian president Shukri al-Quwatli that enabled the construction of an oil pipeline
Law: Congress overrode this president's veto of what act, co-sponsored by Fred Hartley and Robert Taft?
Law: Congress overrode this president's veto of what act, which established the Subversive Activities Control Board?
back: McCarran Internal Security Act (Harry S. Truman)

front: Figure: inflation spiked after Congress abolished the Office of Price Administration over this president's veto
Case: this president's attempt to nationalize the steel industry was struck down as unconstitutional in what Supreme Court case?
back: Youngstown Sheet & Tube Co. v. Sawyer (Harry S. Truman)

front: Figure: this president desegregated the U.S. armed forces
Figure: under this president, Defense Secretary Louis A. Johnson unsuccessfully tried to merge the Marine Corps into the Army
Event: during this president's tenure, at least 20 people were killed when a devastating smog blanketed the Pittsburgh suburb of Donora
Figure: this president fired Attorney General J. Howard McGrath after McGrath fired a special prosecutor investigating IRS corruption for being too zealous
Figure: this president helped save corrupt New York City mayor William O'Dwyer from prosecution by appointing him Ambassador to Mexico
Event: a Chicago (Daily) Tribune edition erroneously printed what headline about the 1948 election, which this president was later photographed holding up?
Event: pollster Gallup undercounted this president's 1948 support partly because it sampled only households with a landline phone
Event: the supposedly psychic horse "Lady Wonder," who claimed to predict elections by ESP, incorrectly predicted Dewey would defeat what president in 1948?
Figure: this president ran his 1948 campaign against the "Do-Nothing" 80th Congress
Event: this president's 1948 campaign financed the first-ever comic book biography of a presidential candidate, depicting "big business" men as cigar-chomping villains
back: Harry S. Truman campaign comic book

front: Event: on November 1, 1950, Puerto Rican nationalists Oscar Collazo and Griselio Torresola attempted to assassinate this president outside what residence?
back: Blair House (attempted assassination of Harry S. Truman)

front: Event: in this 1950 assassination attempt, White House policeman Leslie Coffelt was killed, and the wounded Collazo later had his death sentence commuted
back: Attempted assassination of Harry S. Truman

front: Event: the perpetrators of this 1950 assassination attempt were inspired by the failed revolts of Pedro Albizu Campos
back: Attempted assassination of Harry S. Truman

front: Figure: after surviving an assassination attempt, this president remarked the attackers were "as stupid as they could be"
Figure: this politician threatened to "beat the crap out of" music critic Paul Hume, calling him an "eight ulcer man on four ulcer pay," after Hume panned his daughter's concert
Figure: the White House's second-floor balcony overlooking the South Lawn is named for what president?
Figure: after leaving office, this president denounced Dwight Eisenhower for failing to denounce Joseph McCarthy, and endorsed Adlai Stevenson to succeed him
Law: this act was passed primarily to benefit Harry S. Truman with a pension and other post-presidential support
Figure: this president's namesakes include the mascot of the University of Missouri
